---
title: ""
page-layout: full
toc: false
---

```{=html}
<div class="hero">
  <div class="badge-mono">🛰 ASTRODYNAMICS &amp; ESTIMATION</div>
  <h1>Spacecraft Navigation</h1>
  <p>Interactive tutorials on orbital mechanics, estimation theory, and navigation algorithms — with live visualizations and derivations.</p>
</div>

<div style="display:grid; grid-template-columns: repeat(auto-fill, minmax(220px,1fr)); gap:1.2rem; margin-bottom:2rem;">

  <a class="topic-card" href="least-squares.html">
    <div class="icon">📐</div>
    <h3>Least Squares</h3>
    <p>Fitting models to noisy measurements. The foundation of every navigation filter.</p>
  </a>

  <div class="topic-card" style="opacity:0.5; cursor:not-allowed;">
    <div class="icon">🔭</div>
    <h3>Kalman Filter <span style="font-size:0.7rem; color:#aaa;">(coming soon)</span></h3>
    <p>Recursive optimal estimation for dynamic systems.</p>
  </div>

  <div class="topic-card" style="opacity:0.5; cursor:not-allowed;">
    <div class="icon">🌍</div>
    <h3>Lambert's Problem <span style="font-size:0.7rem; color:#aaa;">(coming soon)</span></h3>
    <p>Solving two-point boundary value problems for orbital transfer.</p>
  </div>

  <div class="topic-card" style="opacity:0.5; cursor:not-allowed;">
    <div class="icon">🛸</div>
    <h3>Orbit Determination <span style="font-size:0.7rem; color:#aaa;">(coming soon)</span></h3>
    <p>Determining orbital elements from tracking measurements.</p>
  </div>

</div>
```

## About This Site

These pages accompany a self-guided study of spacecraft navigation methods. Each tutorial combines:

- **Mathematical derivations** with rendered LaTeX
- **Interactive Plotly visualizations** you can manipulate in-browser
- **Annotated Python code** you can copy and run locally

All visualizations are self-contained — no server required.
